user.name: Santiago
user.description: Computer scientist. I teach hard-core AI/ML Engineering at https://t.co/THCAAZcBMu. YouTube: https://t.co/pROi08OZYJ

fullText:
You should try "Summarize from here" in Claude Code.

I think this is an underrated trick to deal with your ever-growing context.

Basically, instead of using /compact or letting Claude Code to compact your entire session, do the following:

1. Hit Esc+Esc (or type /rewind). This opens the checkpoint menu with every checkpoint Claude created during the session.

2. Pick a checkpoint that came after the context you'd like to keep.

3. Select "Summarize from here."

Everything before that checkpoint will stay exactly as it was. Everything after will get collapsed into a compact summary.

You keep the valuable early context (specs, decisions, constraints) and get rid of the crappy noise.

fullText:
Inflation since 1971 when gold and $ decoupled: today, $1T buys ~4% of all the gold ever mined (ATGEM). In 1971, only $5B were needed. In that sense, a trillionaire today is like a 5-billionaire of 1971, a billionaire like a 5-millionaire, a millionaire like a 5-thousandaire.

Since 1971, ATGEM has roughly doubled. So has the world's population. Gold per human: still roughly 1 ounce (~28g). 

Gold's purchasing power has remained largely unchanged since Roman times, whereas the value of all fiat currencies since then has dropped to zero or almost zero. 

As of 2026, ATGEM would fit into a cube with sides measuring ~22 meters.

Rough estimates of historical market caps of major companies as fractions of ATGEM:

★ 1971: 1 ounce of gold = $35;  IBM market cap $37B ~1/3 of ATGEM

★ 2007: gold $800; Petrochina 1st public $1T company ~1/4 of ATGEM

★ 2010: gold $1100; Saudi Aramco Financial Times estimate $7T > 90% of ATGEM

★ 2019: gold $1560; Aramco after IPO $2T ~1/5 of ATGEM

★ 2022: gold $1800; Apple 1st public $3T company ~1/4 of ATGEM

★ Jul 2025: gold $3300; NVIDIA 1st public $4T company ~1/5 of ATGEM

★ Oct 2025: gold $4000; NVIDIA 1st public $5T company ~1/5 of ATGEM

Massive inflation is the only reason we are talking about trillions now. Recall the 1923 hyperinflation in the Weimar Republic when everyone became a multi-trillionaire.

user.name: Shital Shah
user.description: Mostly research and code. If universe is an optimizer, what is its loss function? All opinions are my own.

fullText:
One big issue with “curing the death” is that our brains are not designed to learn forever. It has limited neurons and like any big model, the learning curve eventually flattens out. If you keep going anyway, forgetting will firmly take hold and then you are essentially only living within a sliding window of ~70 yrs while unable to adapt to new world that keeps developing around you because your neurons got locked into patterns of the past.

If somehow you can solve this problem, you will essentially turn into a new person every x years, almost completely unrelated to who you were before k*x years. 

This then isn’t very different from having biological children. In fact, immortality is worse than having children because you can have many of them vs just you keep transforming into someone else every x years.

The crux here is that we are finite learning machine and infinite time requires digesting infinite entropy.

fullText:
刚好说起这事来了，顺便说一下推特上应该怎么转发别人的内容。

如果你想做大，想做国际化的话，可以看看。毕竟海外的一些人，他不惯着你。

如果说有原文，你最好是转发。然后呢你可以截他的原文在你的转发的推文上，这个一般大家是不会说你的。

然后如果是视频，你可以长按去引用视频，然后在第二条推串去引用他的原推文，这样是相当于通知他，那么他如果觉得这样不好，他会来找你，那么你删掉就可以。

然后你如果艾特了别人，然后直接截图的话，也可以在推串的第二条贴上它的原文。

这个其实不影响你的阅读量，也分不了你多少流量。就是要有共赢思维，不要想着说把任何事情任何流量要吃干抹净。

如果说你转发了他的内容，然后他不喜欢，他觉得你转发的方式不恰当的话，你也可以跟他沟通，然后首先删掉，要不就是在推串上来一个解释和声明。

这些操作其实可以让你的涨粉越来越快，让你的朋友越来越多。当然，如果你的目的不是想做公域流量，那另说。

fullText:
GALBOT ROBOTICS: CHINA’S EMBODIED AI BREAKTHROUGH

In 2025, we had the “DeepSeek moment”.

A Chinese startup shocked the AI world with its high performance, open source, low cost model. 

Then 2 months ago, we had the “Seedance moment.”

Low cost Video generation reached Hollywood levels of quality.

Now it’s the “Galbot moment”

These are Robots with embodied AI. 

That work 24/7

That will be low cost.

And they have already been deployed by the thousands in Chinese retail, healthcare and logistics.

fullText:
HUAWEI'S ENGINEERS PUSH HIGH-TECH LIMITS IN CONSUMER ELECTRONICS

I recently went to the huawei consumer product innovation forum in Bangkok.

Here are my takeaways. 

1: Huawei does continuous hardware innovation across multiple device types. 

This enables proprietary features across smartphones, wearables, and tablets to build advantages that competitors cannot match.

2: The PaperMatte display feels like writing on paper.

This is based on a two-step manufacturing process. 

First, the screen uses chemical etching to scatter light and create pen friction.

Then magnetron sputtering of optical coatings restores 3K resolution clarity.

3: Huawei’s new 4.7mm thin tablet has six speakers.

It uses integrated AI and audio tech to overcome the physical limits of a thin chassis. 

Read here for more.

https://t.co/L1eHgjAWEo
